description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/sestatus.conf, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/sestatus.conf
rationale:
Setting correct permissions on the /etc/sestatus.conf file is important
because this file hosts SELinux configuration. Protection of this
file is critical for system security. Restricting the permissions
ensures exclusive control of the SELinux configuration.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/sestatus.conf, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/sestatus.conf
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: 0644
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/sestatus.conf" file to "0644" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/sestatus.conf
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/sestatus.conf file must have mode 0644 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.
title: Verify Permissions On /etc/sestatus.conf File
